rules.rule: Align props vertically when more than two props are present, one per line
rules.description: Multi-prop JSX elements should have each prop on its own line, aligned with the first prop

rules.rule: Use template literals or cn() for dynamic class names; keep static strings as plain string literals
rules.description: Static class strings stay as double-quoted literals; dynamic classes use cn() calls

rules.rule: Place each argument on its own line when there are more than two arguments
rules.description: Multi-argument cn() calls should have each argument on a separate line for readability

rules.rule: Format variant keys and values with each variant axis as a separate block, values indented within
rules.description: The variants object in cva() should clearly delineate each axis (variant, size) as indented blocks

rules.rule: Group imports in order: React/Next → third-party (Radix, zod, react-hook-form) → @/components/ui/* → @/lib/utils → relative imports
rules.description: Imports should follow a consistent grouping order with blank lines between groups

rules.rule: Order props: ref → key → variant/size → className → event handlers → spread props → children
rules.description: Maintain consistent prop ordering across shadcn components

rules.rule: Align variant class strings to the same indentation level within a cva() definition
rules.description: Variant value strings should be visually aligned for quick scanning

rules.rule: Nest FormItem > FormLabel > FormControl > FormDescription > FormMessage with consistent 2-space indentation
rules.description: Form field sub-components follow a strict nesting hierarchy

rules.rule: Order Tailwind classes: layout (flex, grid) → sizing (w, h) → spacing (p, m) → typography → colors → borders → effects → states (hover, focus)
rules.description: Follow a consistent Tailwind utility class ordering convention

rules.rule: Indent JSX children by 2 spaces; closing tags align with opening tags
rules.description: JSX blocks use 2-space indentation with closing tags at the same level as their opening counterpart
